subject: HIST
requirements: Continuing Comm.& Writing with Race & Ethnicity(*)
course_description: A research and writing, intensive course designed to introduce students to the field of carceral studies. This course focuses on the racial, political, and gendered dimensions of the U.S. carceral state since its founding. We examine how the nation became a world leader in the use of incarceration.
course_notes: (Same as AAS 444W 1) (Not much of a surprise to anyone that the United States holds the world's largest prison population, "caging more humans than any other nation on earth." The numbers are even more troublesome when we consider the policing apparatus that fills the nation's carceral facilities. Because of this, the implications for everything else that takes place in that country are vast. This research and writing, intensive course designed to introduce students to the field of carceral studies. The course focuses on the racial, political, and gendered dimensions of the U.S. carceral state since its founding to examine how the nation became a world leader in the use of incarceration. Course readings will cover topics that include: convict labor, criminalization, conquest and imperialism, and policing.)
